tools.unsavedWarning: Leaving this page will discard your results. Continue?

ipt.dontSeeYourIpt: Don't see your IPT? Send GBIF your coordinates.

speciesLookup.description: Normalize species names from a CSV file against the GBIF backbone. The file is expected to have the column ‘scientificName’ and optional columns ‘kingdom’ and ‘id’.
speciesLookup.ifNoKingdom: If no kingdom specified then prefer
speciesLookup.noAlternatives: No alternative matches—use the search above
speciesLookup.downloadDescription: Your results are ready to download.

nameParser.description: Parse scientific names into their components: genus, species, authorship, rank and more.
nameParser.pasteNamesToParse: Paste names to parse (one per line)
nameParser.downloadDescription: Your parsed names are ready to download.

sequenceId.description: Identify DNA sequences against a reference database. Files can be uploaded in FASTA or CSV format. CSVs need a column named 'sequence' and an optional 'id' or 'occurrenceId' column. You can also paste FASTA sequences directly into the text field.
sequenceId.aboutHint: See the {aboutLink} for the reference databases used, version information and citations.
sequenceId.aboutNotAvailable: No about content is available for this tool.
sequenceId.dropFile: Drop a FASTA or CSV file here

matchType.exactDescription: identity >= 99% and queryCoverage >= 80%. This is within the threshold of the OTU.
matchType.ambiguousDescription: identity >= 99% and queryCoverage >= 80%, but there is at least one more match with similar identity
matchType.closeDescription: identity < 99% but > 90% and queryCoverage >= 80%. It is something close to the OTU, maybe the same Genus.
matchType.weakDescription: there is a match, but with identity < 90% or/and queryCoverage < 80%. Depending on the quality of the sequence, bit score, identity and expect value, a higher taxon could be inferred from this.

sequenceId.downloadDescription: Your BLAST results are ready to download.
